title: More or Less Fuel
description: Originally a song I made for an 8th Grade project. Kind of... Obviously the date on this is nowhere near when I was in 8th Grade because this is actually an extended version I made WAYYY after the fact. The original "theme" was that little riff you year at the end and nothing else. I came up with the rest at an eSports center that went out of business years back. *TANGENT APPROACHING* ... All I gotta say about that is my man should've picked to put his center by the Walmart in the opening he was referring to, who cares if you cannot sell food, there's some right next door. Plus, you get far more foot traffic from kids and high schoolers seeing your establishment after being dragged along for errands by their guardians than probably a bunch of broke college students walking out of a thrift store and catching a glimpse of an eSports place in what is effectively the middle of nowhere. No hard feelings Mr. Gene, I liked your business, I've been following you ever since you were a little merchandise store at the local mall and I felt betrayed to hear how quickly things went south for you. Only 3 people probably know what I'm talking about but man it still haunts me.

title: Steamed Gadget Zone

title: The Thinkarounderer

title: Day 25 of Summer (2024 Remake)
description: A very interesting idea popped into my head. Despite me being distant from my whole Substantial Sandbox idea, I thought it wouldn't hurt to give Day 25 of Summer a rethink, especially with it being pretty significant as an original track. I still absolutely love what I did to this, I think it's so genius and the jazz section was such a good improvement. Yes, that is a lot of complementing on my part, but it's like rekindling a relationship.

title: Sora Made Jumping - Ojamajo Doremi (SRB2 Special Stage 8)
description: I was requested to make a midi rendition of Sora Made Jumping, a-la the SRB2 Demo 4 Special Stages. It was pretty meticulous trying to transcribe everything. The song was so dynamic, I used a stem splitter to make sense of it all, crazy stuff.

title: Jigga Juice - Concert Version
description: The last song you'd ever see me make, and that's the whole joke. I know too much explanation kills humor but I cannot resist telling you why this is even a thing. For some reason, one of the most prominent gameday songs my high school band director used to hype us up was Jigga Juice, sure I guess it... broadly works(?) anyways, fast forward over to symphonic band season, where we're playing all this slow and pretty stuff and all of a sudden, a certain somebody who would soon become drum captain suggested that we play Jigga Juice for the Concert Program. Yes we're gonna get such high scores with that. I thought it was so amusing that I made this, so you're welcome! (He actually doesn't even know I made this)

title: The Meeting of Time
description: I wanted to make a song that sounded like meeting yourself but from the past... I don't know man, add this to a homecoming playlist or something.

title: Sonic Origins - Cutscene (TIMOTHY ESCAPED)
description: A midi I made to go along with dialogue for a joke fangame I was making with The Games Factory called Sonic Origins. NO, not the compilation, I mean some 12 year old's interpretation of Sonic's Origins, and The 8-Bit Guy is there for some reason. Check the preview bar to hear what it sounds like WITH the dialogue.

title: Double Stroke
description:
I don't mean "double stoke" in the medical sense, I mean it in the rudimental drumming sense. Yeah, dug out of that hole... Anyways, this piece does have some "lyrics" to which I did not make the song with them in mind. I just kind of started singing them while hearing this song and that's why I called it what it is.

Trying to work on my double stroke (Whooaah)
Sticks just thuddin' like it's a joke (it's a joke)
How should I ever keep the beat (Whooaah)
When I play on the street?

I do think they're stupid, but whatever.

title: 1979 Honor Band Song
description: I do not know who wrote this. The title says all you need to know, it's from a tape I own that says "1979 Honor Stage Band" on the front. It's a really fun song, but I would very much like to find who did it.

title: Mochimochi (Incomplete)
description: I forget who exactly suggested this song to me and where, but I remember liking it and deciding to go forward with de-making it into midi. Way harder than I thought it would be, and now here we are. Am I going to de-make any other Vocaloid-centric music? -- Probably. I just don't know who I'd be making it for. Maybe I'll complete this if I'm feeling adventurous with a lot of time on my hands or if someone tells me that their soul will forever be haunted by the fact that this isn't finished. Chances of the latter are slim though.

title: Egg Rock Zone Act 1 - Vertz1515
artist: Big Wave Dave, Clairebun, & Shane Mychal Sexton
description: After MONTHS (and I mean months!) of people requesting me to do an Egg Rock Zone Act 1 legacy remix. I finally caved and went in, however, I think we may be staying a bit from the *authentic* legacy feel, I'd say... but I'm not complaining, I love how this turned out.

description: I just thought the song sounded interesting, and I wanted to take the opportunity to recreate the Pac-Man death sound in midi. Just having a fun, bored old time.

title: Chattanooga Choo Choo
artist: Harry Warren, Mack Gordon, & Bob Cotter
description: Really liked this piece. I know the original song, but I especially liked the arrangement Bob Cotter did, which I heard on a vinyl. This was supposed to be a YM2612 chiptune piece but Deflemask crashed before I got meaningful work done, thus, I pivoted.
